concept: Attach or modify a sugar (glycoside) at a peripheral position to tune selectivity between closely related target isoforms, without altering the core pharmacophore.
mechanism: The sugar moiety modulates binding kinetics and affinity across target isoforms (e.g. Na+/K+-ATPase alpha-1 vs alpha-2/alpha-3), shifting selectivity; the aglycone core keeps its intrinsic activity.
precedent_drug: Digitalis glycosides (e.g. digitoxin) versus their aglycones

citation.label: Katz A, Lifshitz Y, Bab-Dinitz E, Kapri-Pardes E, Goldshleger R, Tal DM, Karlish SJD. Selectivity of digitalis glycosides for isoforms of human Na,K-ATPase. J Biol Chem. 2010;285(25):19582-92.
citation.note: The sugar group governs isoform selectivity; the aglycone is largely non-selective. Also in the flagship research log.
